data.title: REG - Nexteq PLC - Transaction in Own Shares and Total Voting Rights
data.description:
RNS Number : 3610A Nexteq PLC 12 March 2025  

12 March 2025

Nexteq plc

("Nexteq" or the "Group")

Transaction in Own Shares

and

Total Voting Rights

Nexteq PLC (the "Company") announces that on 11 March 2025 it purchased 30,000 ordinary shares of 0.1 pence each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") at a price of 68.00 pence each, to be held in treasury.

Further to the above transaction, the Company advises that its issued share capital stands at 66,539,060 Ordinary Shares, with 6,486,398 Ordinary Shares held in treasury. Consequently, the total voting rights of the Company is 60,052,662.

The above voting rights figure of 60,052,662 may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

data.title: DAX Rebounds on Wednesday
data.description:
The DAX rose more than 0.5% to around 22,475 on Wednesday, after three consecutive sessions of decreases, in line with most European peers.

Market sentiment was driven by optimism over a potential 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine.

Meanwhile, the European Union said Wednesday it would impose "countermeasures" from April 1 in response to "injustified" US tariffs of 25% on steel and aluminium imports.

Industrials, banks and construction materials posted the biggest gains.

On the downside, Porsche declined about 0.3% after its sales revenue and operating profit fell, while Puma tumbled more than 20% after delivering a weaker FY forecasts.

data.title: Deye Technology to Invest 88 Million Yuan in Venture Capital Fund
data.description:
Ningbo Deye Technology Group is investing 88 million yuan in venture capital fund Ningbo Derun Taihe Venture Capital Partnership.

The solar inverter manufacturer's investment represents an 88% stake in the fund's 100-million-yuan capital.

The fund will focus on robotics, artificial intelligence, semiconductors, new materials and renewable energy.

data.title: Zhejiang Yonggui Electric Equipment to Issue 980 Million Yuan Convertible Corporate Bonds
data.description:
Zhejiang Yonggui Electric Equipment plans to issue 980 million yuan in convertible corporate bonds, according to a Tuesday filing with the Shenzhen Bourse.

The China Securities Regulatory Commission approved the issuance. The bonds, with a six-year term and annual interest rates increasing from 0.2% to 2.3%, will be listed on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

Existing shareholders have priority for subscription, with the remaining available for public investors.

Notes to Editors:

About discoverIE Group plc

discoverIE Group plc is an international group of businesses that design and manufacture innovative electronic components for industrial applications.

The Group provides application-specific components to original equipment manufacturers ("OEMs") internationally through its two divisions, Magnetics & Controls, and Sensing & Connectivity. By designing components that meet customers' unique requirements, which are then manufactured and supplied throughout the life of their production, a high level of repeating revenue is generated with long-term, high quality customer relationships.

With a focus on key markets driven by structural growth, increasing electronic content and sustainability, namely medical, electrification of transportation, renewable energy, security and industrial automation & connectivity, the Group aims to achieve organic growth that is well ahead of GDP and to supplement that with complementary acquisitions. The Group is committed to reducing the impact of its operations on the environment in order to reach net zero. With its key markets aligned with a sustainable future, the Group has been awarded an ESG "AA" rating by MSCI and is Regional (Europe) Top Rated by Sustainalytics. 

The Group employs c.4,500 people across 20 countries with its principal operating units located in Continental Europe, the UK, China, Sri Lanka, India and North America.

discoverIE is listed on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange and is a member of the FTSE 250, classified within the Electrical Components and Equipment subsector.

data.title: Chip, Battery Stock Gains Push South Korean Shares to Close Higher; Samsung Adds Over 2%, Sk Hynix 6%
data.description:
South Korean shares closed higher on Wednesday on gains made by chipmakers and battery stocks. In addition, data indicating a decline in the jobless rate in the country in February also boosted investor sentiment.

The Korea Composite Stock Price Index, or Kospi, was up by 37.22 points, or 1.47%, to close at 2,574.82. The Kosdaq was also up by 7.99 points, or 1.11%, to 729.49.

Shares of key market movers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix increased more than 2% and nearly 6% at market close.

In economic news, the seasonally adjusted unemployment rate in South Korea in February fell to 2.7% from 2.8% in the year-ago period, data from Statistics Korea indicated Wednesday.

The jobless rate also declined from 2.9% in January.

The number of unemployed people rose by 25,000 to 940,000 in February from a year earlier. The number of employed people increased by 136,000 to hit 28.2 million last month from the year-ago period.

The labor force participation rate added 0.1 percentage points to 63.7% last month from February 2024.

In corporate news, Samsung Electronics made its largest-ever research and development and facility investments in 2024, spending 35 trillion won on R&D, up 23.5% year on year, and 53.6 trillion won on facility expansion.

The increased spending came amid Samsung seeking new growth strategies amid intensifying competition and declining global demand for memory chips.

Despite a dip in its TV, smartphone, and DRAM market shares, the company posted 300.87 trillion won in revenue last year, marking a 16.2% increase and the second-highest annual figure ever.

Shares of Samsung Electronics rose more than 2% at market close on Wednesday.

data.title: Renova's February Electricity Sales Rise 51%
data.description:
Renova reported February electricity sales of 257.8 million kWh, exceeding its target by 2.1% and marking nearly a 51% year-on-year increase, according to a Wednesday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The rise was driven by the addition of the Omaezakikou Biomass plant, which boosted its total biomass capacity to 395.3 MW.

Year-to-date electricity sales reached 2.29 billion kWh, inline with its plan but 4% higher than the previous year.

The impact of output curtailments remained minor, accounting for just 0.062% of the annual revenue plan.

data.title: UK Market Talk Roundup: Shares Losing
data.description:
Stocks on the fall in the UK today. Compiled by Dow Jones Newswires Markets Desk, markets.eu@dowjones.com

Europe's defense companies face the risk of profit taking in the near-term if Ukraine and Russia reach a cease-fire agreement, after years of consistent share-price increases, Barclays analysts say. Defense has outperformed MSCI Europe by about 60% year-to-date, and investors are now questioning whether multiples have reached bubble territory, the analysts write. Expected good news on rising defense spending is likely priced in now, they say. However, they recommend using potential dips as buying opportunities, given the strong structural growth prospects of European defense companies. Shares in Leonardo are down 3.4%, while Thales falls 6.5% at 228 euros, and BAE Systems loses 2% at 15.44 pounds. Indra Sistemas, QinetiQ, Saab, Dassault Aviation, Fincantieri, Chemring, Babcock International and Hensoldt are also down in morning trade. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

Contact us in London. +44-20-7842-9288
Markettalk.eu@dowjones.com

data.title: Potential Ukraine Cease-Fire Raises Prospect of Profit Taking in Defense Sector — Market Talk
data.description:
0840 GMT - Europe's defense companies face the risk of profit taking in the near-term if Ukraine and Russia reach a cease-fire agreement, after years of consistent share-price increases, Barclays analysts say. Defense has outperformed MSCI Europe by about 60% year-to-date, and investors are now questioning whether multiples have reached bubble territory, the analysts write. Expected good news on rising defense spending is likely priced in now, they say. However, they recommend using potential dips as buying opportunities, given the strong structural growth prospects of European defense companies. Shares in Leonardo are down 3.4%, while Thales falls 6.5% at 228 euros, and BAE Systems loses 2% at 15.44 pounds. Indra Sistemas, QinetiQ, Saab, Dassault Aviation, Fincantieri, Chemring, Babcock International and Hensoldt are also down in morning trade. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0830 GMT - Edmond de Rothschild Asset Management sticks to a neutral position on fixed income markets, says global CIO Benjamin Melman in a note. This partly reflects a low term premium on the U.S. Treasury market, in addition to rising uncertainty and stretched equity valuation, he says. Term premium is the additional yield investors demand to hold a longer-dated bond over a shorter-dated one. The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield falls 1.5 basis points to 4.272%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0819 GMT - The price of the March 2035 gilt--which is due to be auctioned at 1000 GMT--has declined since the bond was launched via syndication on February 11, RBC Capital Markets analysts say in a note. "The strong post-launch performance observed on the previous two 10-year conventional gilts on relative value basis is not being replicated in the March 2035 gilt thus far," they say. The bond is likely to cheapen further ahead of the auction, RBC says. "Given the level of macro uncertainty in the current environment and the relatively poor performance of the [gilt] on micro-relative value since launch, we would anticipate a further auction concession this morning." (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0816 GMT - The Canadian dollar falls as investors fret over U.S.-Canada trade tensions and await a highly-anticipated interest-rate cut by the Bank of Canada at 1345 GMT. President Trump briefly threatened to double tariffs on Canadian steel and aluminium imports before walking back on this plan. A 25% tariff took effect Wednesday. While Trump's tariff threat only lasted half a day, developments point to a further escalation in the U.S.-Canada trade spat, ING analyst Francesco Pesole says in a note. It raises the possibility the BOC will signal further rate cuts to prevent excessive negative repricing in growth expectations, he says. The U.S. dollar rises 0.1% to 1.4454 Canadian dollars after hitting a one-week high of 1.4521 Tuesday, according to FactSet. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0756 GMT - Key U.S. inflation data are due at 1230 GMT, where a weaker-than-expected core inflation reading could add to expectations that the U.S. Federal Reserve will cut interest rates three times this year, says CMC's Jochen Stanzl in a note. Such a reading could help boost U.S. stocks, although likely only temporarily as focus switches back to tariff fears, the chief market analyst says. "The initial joy over favorable inflation data might quickly give way to renewed skepticism, as there is now widespread acknowledgment that tariffs could have inflationary effects." U.S. money markets fully price in three rate cuts in 2025, having priced in only one to two earlier this year, LSEG data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0755 GMT - The euro falls as trade tensions rise after the EU announced 26 billion euros worth of retaliatory measures in response to President Trump's tariffs on steel and aluminium imports that took effect Wednesday. European Commission chief, Ursula von der Leyen, said the 25% U.S. tariffs were "unjustified" and announced a series of countermeasures for April 1. These measures could "temporarily knock the euro off course," XTB's Kathleen Brooks says in a note.The euro falls 0.2% to $1.0897, having reached a near five-month high of $1.0949 Tuesday, according to FactSet.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0743 GMT - Eurozone government bond yields are little changed in early trade, stabilizing after Tuesday's selloff, according to Tradeweb data. The day ahead is eventful, however, and could spark volatility later. The European Central Bank's "ECB and Its Watchers" conference takes place in Frankfurt, while bond supply is due from Germany and Portugal, the latter one day after the government's collapse in a no-confidence vote. Key U.S. inflation data are due at 1230 GMT. U.S. President Trump's steel and aluminum tariffs take effect, while the EU announced retaliatory tariffs early Wednesday, effective April. The 10-year German Bund yield falls 0.4 basis point to 2.890%, having risen about 8 basis points on Tuesday, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0738 GMT - The Bank of Japan is likely to keep policy settings unchanged next week, but with inflation set to overshoot its forecasts, there is a strong chance for a rate hike in May rather than in July as most anticipate, says Marcel Thieliant at Capital Economics. BOJ Deputy Gov. Uchida gave no indication that a hike is forthcoming in his speech last week, says CE's head of Asia-Pacific. Financial markets are pricing just a 4% chance of a 25bp rate hike next week, he says. However, the case for further policy tightening has continued to strengthen as inflation and wage growth have accelerated. With inflation set to remain around the BOJ's 2% target for longer, CE now expects the bank to lift rates to 1.5% by 2027. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0731 GMT - The dollar continues to fall on expectations of a sharp U.S. economic slowdown that could potentially lead to a recession in the second half, Swissquote Bank analyst Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a note. The market expects the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates in June, according to LSEG. However, an acceleration of the market selloff could pull the timeline to May, she says. U.S. inflation data at 12:30 GMT will be key. Softer-than-expected data would give the Federal Reserve a bigger margin to cut rates while higher-than-forecast numbers would reduce the scope of cuts, she says. The DXY dollar index falls 0.2% to 103.652, having reached a 20-week low of 103.221 Tuesday. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0731 GMT - Sweden's National Debt Office will reopen the March 2039-dated government bond at Wednesday's auction for the first time since 2019, offering 1 billion kronor in this debt. While not auctioned by the debt office since 2019, the bond has been frequently sold at Riksbank's auctions under its quantitative tightening, Danske Bank Research strategists say in a note. In the latest auction last week, this bond saw a bid-to-cover of 4.8, "which suggests that there should be good demand at today's auction too," they say. At Wednesday's auction, the National Debt Office will also offer 4 billion kronor in November 2029-dated bonds. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0712 GMT - The resilience of Portuguese bonds to the government losing a vote of confidence Tuesday is a proof of the strong performance of eurozone periphery debt, Danske Bank Research's strategists say. Portugal now faces a snap election, possibly in May. "However, it did not have much impact on the spread between Portuguese government bonds and matching German government bonds," Kristoffer Kjaer Lomholt and Filip Andersson say. This also highlights the strong performance of eurozone peripheral government bonds, they say. Portugal will hold a bond auction Wednesday but Danske's strategists don't expect the political uncertainty to have much impact on it. On Tuesday, the 10-year Portuguese-German yield spread closed at 50 basis points, down about 1.3 basis points, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0708 GMT - The ECB's approach to launch a digital euro reflects the bloc's consensus-driven model and regulatory oversight, which contrasts with hesitant developments in the U.S. "I think there's a fundamental belief that the private sector is much better placed to be the innovator around digital money topics," Deutsche Bank's head of digital assets and currencies transformation Sabih Behzad says in a panel at the MoneyLIVE Summit in London on the U.S.'s approach, which he sees as driven by privacy and anonymity concerns. Nordea's chief strategist for digital currencies Ville Sointu adds that these divergences underline how central bank digital currencies are "extremely political projects": China's digital yuan is highly controlled and centralised while Nigeria's ambition is to use digital currencies for financial inclusion. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Jefferies starts coverage on India's Swiggy with 'hold'
data.description:
** Jefferies initiates Swiggy with "hold" rating, PT of 400 rupees

** Food and grocery delivery platform's market share of 45% in food delivery to grow in high-teens in medium term with margin expansion, says Jefferies

** Says quick commerce offers "tremendous growth opportunity" although faces high competition and thus SWIG's profitability will remain under pressure

** Jefferies expects SWIG to remain in losses over FY25-27, competition from new entrants Flipkart and Amazon to result in loss trend persisting

** Stock down 0.8% at 350.20 rupees, set for fourth straight losing session

** Avg rating on SWIG, rival Zomato at "buy" - data compiled by LSEG

** SWIG down 35% so far this year vs ZOMT's 26% decline

data.title: Stressed market conditions reinstated in derivatives on ZEAL (61/25)
data.description:
NASDAQ NordicProduct information

Stressed market conditions reinstated in derivatives on ZEAL (61/25)

Nasdaq has decided to call for stressed market conditions in derivatives onZealand Pharma AS (ZEAL) for four hours, after which time an assessment ofmarket conditions will be made to determine if stressed market conditions arestill valid.

Stressed market conditions in derivatives is called for when the underlyingasset to a derivative experience high and short term intraday volatility orwhen other factors causes high uncertainty in the pricing of derivatives. Whenstressed market is called for it means that market makers are allowed to quoteprices with double the normal spread.

For contact details please see attached file.https://view.news.eu.nasdaq.com/view?id=bbdf050fcb3af234fa349064cd57a625d&lang=en&src=omxlink

data.title: Most Gulf markets rise on Ukraine ceasefire hopes
data.description:
Most major stock markets in the Gulf rose in early trade on Wednesday, boosted by Ukraine's readiness to support a proposal for a 30-day ceasefire with Russia, although investors remained cautious about the impact of U.S. tariffs.

Saudi Arabia's benchmark index nudged 0.1% higher, helped by a 2.7% rise in Dr Sulaiman Al Habib Medical Services .

Oil giant Saudi Aramco eased 0.2%. Fitch Ratings expects Saudi Arabia's deficit to widen as Aramco's dividend normalizes.

Dubai's main share index gained 0.4%, with blue-chip developer Emaar Properties rising 1.2% and sharia-compliant lender Dubai Islamic Bank putting on 1.9%.

In Abu Dhabi, the index added 0.1%.

Investors are waiting for U.S. Consumer Price Index data at 1230 GMT to gauge the Federal Reserve's next move on interest rates.

Monetary policy in the six-member Gulf Cooperation Council is usually guided by the Fed's decisions, as most regional currencies are pegged to the dollar.

The Qatari index fell 0.6%, weighed down by a 5.5% slide in telecom firm Ooredoo , as the stock traded ex-dividend.

data.title: Denali Capital Acquisition Pushes Merger Deadline With Extra Funding
data.description:
Denali Capital Acquisition said Tuesday it has moved its business combination deadline to April 11 and has placed $15,063 into its trust fund.

The extension was backed by a zero-interest note from Scilex Holding , which allows Denali to borrow $180,000, repayable upon either a completed deal or liquidation, and convertible into class A shares at $10 per share, the company said.

Denali said the remaining balance of $59,545 under the note can be used to fund another monthly extensions if needed.

data.title: Al Khaleej Training Unit Lands SAR15 Million Customer Service Center Operations, Management Contract
data.description:
Al Khaleej Training and Education's (SASE:4290) Advanced Communications Systems and Solutions Co. - Smart Link secured a one-year operations and management contract from the Saudi Commission For Health Specialties.

Under the 14.7 million-Saudi-riyal contract, the Saudi Arabia-based training and education company subsidiary will handle, manage, and boost the client's customer service center, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: EHang Holdings Swings to Adjusted Earnings in Q4 as Sales More Than Double
data.description:
EHang Holdings reported Q4 adjusted earnings Wednesday of 0.50 renminbi ($0.07) per diluted American depository share, compared with a loss of 0.34 renminbi a year earlier.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was 164.3 million renminbi, up from 56.6 million renminbi a year earlier.

Three analysts surveyed by FactSet expected 170.2 million renminbi.

The company expects fiscal 2025 revenue of 900 million renminbi. Analysts polled by FactSet expect 859.5 million renminbi.

Shares of the company were 2.5% higher in premarket activity.

data.title: REG - Societe Generale SA - Stabilisation Notice - LEGRAND
data.description:
RNS Number : 3609A Societe Generale SA 12 March 2025  

12th March 2025

Not for distribution, directly or indirectly, in or into the United States or any jurisdiction in which such distribution would be unlawful.

Legrand SA

Pre-stabilisation Period Announcement

Societe Generale (contact: Nicole Bezzant +44 20 7676 8656) hereby gives notice, as Stabilisation Coordinator, that the Stabilisation Manager(s) named below may stabilise the offer of the following securities in accordance with [Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 under the Market Abuse Regulation (EU/596/2014)][and] [the UK FCA Stabilisation Binding Technical Standards].

Securities

Issuer:

Legrand SA

Guarantor(s) (if any):

N/A

Aggregate nominal amount:

EUR 500m

Description:

Coupon:

Maturity:

Offer price:

Stabilisation:

Stabilisation Manager(s)

SG CIB

Stabilisation period expected to start on

12/03/2025

Stabilisation period expected to end no later than

18/04/2025

Existence, maximum size and conditions of use of over‑allotment facility:

The Stabilisation Manager(s) may over‑allot the securities to the extent permitted in accordance with applicable law.

In connection with the offer of the above securities, the Stabilisation Manager(s) may over‑allot10 the securities or effect transactions with a view to supporting the market price of the securities during the stabilisation period at a level higher than that which might otherwise prevail. However, stabilisation may not necessarily occur and any stabilisation action, if begun, may cease at any time11. Any stabilisation action or over‑allotment shall be conducted in accordance with all applicable laws and rules.

This announcement is for information purposes only and does not constitute an invitation or offer to underwrite, subscribe for or otherwise acquire or dispose of any securities of the Issuer in any jurisdiction.

This announcement and the offer of the securities to which it relates are only addressed to and directed at persons outside the United Kingdom and persons in the United Kingdom who have professional experience in matters related to investments or who are high net worth persons within Article 12(5) of the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Financial Promotion) Order 2005 and must not be acted on or relied on by other persons in the United Kingdom12.

In addition, if and to the extent that this announcement is communicated in, or the offer of the securities to which it relates is made in, [the UK] [any EEA Member State] before the publication of a prospectus in relation to the securities which has been approved by the competent authority in [the UK]  [that Member State] in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2017/1129 [as it forms part of domestic  law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 201813] (the "[EU][UK] Prospectus Regulation") [(or which has been approved by a competent authority in another Member State and notified to the competent authority that Member State in accordance with the EU Prospectus Regulation)], this announcement and the offer are only addressed to and directed at persons in [the UK]  [that Member State] who are qualified investors within the meaning of the [EU][UK] Prospectus Regulation (or who are other persons to whom the offer may lawfully be addressed) and must not be acted on or relied on by other persons in [the UK] [that Member State]14.

This announcement is not an offer of securities for sale into the United States. The securities have not been, and will not be, registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933 and may not be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an exemption from registration. There will be no public offer of securities in the United States.

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact rns@lseg.com or visit www.rns.com.

RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our Privacy Policy.  END  STAEAPDAFDFSEEA

data.title: Vietnam, Singapore agree to boost ties, cooperation on subsea cables
data.description:
Singapore and Vietnam on Wednesday have agreed to enhance cooperation in subsea cables, finance, and energy, marking an upgrade in their relations to Vietnam's highest level, during a visit by its Communist Party Chief To Lam to the city-state.

Singapore is the third Southeast Asian nation, after Malaysia and Indonesia, with which Vietnam has established a "comprehensive strategic relationship".

In a joint statement released following the upgrade, Lam and Singapore's Prime Minister Lawrence Wong witnessed the exchange of six agreements and discussed cooperation in undersea cable development, digital connectivity, and cross-border data flows.

Southeast Asian countries, a major junction for cables connecting Asia to Europe, aim to expand their networks to meet the surging demand for AI services and data centres. Vietnam alone plans to launch 10 new submarine cables by 2030.

In December, Reuters reported that Singaporean asset manager Keppel and Vietnamese conglomerate Sovico Group were discussing plans for new undersea fiber-optic cables to boost the region's data centre industry, according to sources familiar with the matter.

In April last year, Vietnam's state-owned telecom company Viettel and Singapore's Singtel announced a preliminary agreement to develop an undersea cable linking Vietnam directly to Singapore, although no construction contract has been announced yet.

The two leaders also discussed green development, industrial parks expansion, and peace and stability in the region. Singapore pledged to support Vietnam in developing international financial centres, the joint statement said.

Singapore ranks among Vietnam's top foreign investors, having invested $10.21 billion last year, which accounted for 27% of Vietnam's total foreign investment, official data showed.

data.title: Low Term Premium on U.S. Treasurys Justify a Neutral Weighting — Market Talk
data.description: Edmond de Rothschild Asset Management sticks to a neutral position on fixed income markets, says global CIO Benjamin Melman in a note. This partly reflects a low term premium on the U.S. Treasury market, in addition to rising uncertainty and stretched equity valuation, he says. Term premium is the additional yield investors demand to hold a longer-dated bond over a shorter-dated one. The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield falls 1.5 basis points to 4.272%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: NIMBUS GROUP APPOINTS FOUR NEW DEALERS IN FRANCE
data.description:
Nimbus Group AB (publ) has appointed four new dealers for Nimbus brand boats in France. The new dealers will act as sub-dealers for Nimbus Group's French dealer Martini Performance. Martini Performance is already a successful dealer of Nimbus brand boats in Corsica and has also established itself in Malta.

The four new dealers are well spread across France, from the Bay of Biscay in the north to Montpellier on the Mediterranean in the south.

"Appointing new dealers in key markets in Europe is central to supporting our growth strategy," says Nicklas Bergström, Head of Nimbus Group's business area Commercial Sales.

data.title: The CAC 40 Index Opens 0.74% Higher
data.description:
In Paris, the CAC 40 Index is picking up 59 points or 0.74 percent on Wednesday.

Gains are led by BNP Paribas (1.79%), Cap Gemini (1.77%) and Saint-Gobain (1.71%).

Biggest losers are Thales (-2.85%), STMicroelectronics (-2.02%) and Publicis (-1.31%).
